sections.about: This metric measures discharge to seawater under BRSR Principle 6 Essential Indicator water discharge by destination. For inland Indian steel operations it is generally expected to be zero; for coastal or overseas sites it can be material. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: All four companies report zero discharge to seawater across all three years. No seawater discharge is expected for landlocked integrated steel operations in India; this metric is relevant primarily for coastal or desalination contexts.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2025, Tata is tied 1st with all peers at zero. Tata is tied 1st across all three years.
sections.targets: Tata’s broader ZED and recycling strategy remains directionally relevant.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Tata Steel FY2022-23 water discharge metrics were not explicitly reported on a purely standalone basis but rather collectively for Tata Steel India and its Indian subsidiaries.
